title: Public idea sharing - read-only URL
description:
Generate a public shareable link for a specific idea using a UUID (unguessable, not sequential). Read-only view with no auth required.

SHARING:
- URL format: /share/<uuid-v4> — cryptographically random, not guessable
- Toggle on/off per idea (revoke = new UUID generated if re-enabled, old link dies)
- Visitor sees exactly what the author sees: title, summary, design doc, tags, vibe, complexity, excitement, next steps, notes

FEEDBACK:
- Simple reaction strip (thumbs up, lightbulb, fire — no accounts needed)
- Flat comment thread — multiple commentors, all comments visible to anyone with the link
- Commentor enters a name (no account/email required)
- Each comment up to 2000 chars
- Author can reply inline (flat, no nesting)
- Rate limited by IP: 1 comment per IP per idea per hour to prevent spam
- No public listing of shared ideas (no discovery surface)

NOTIFICATIONS:
- In-app only — author sees a badge/count on the idea card when new comments arrive
- Author can view and reply to all comments from the idea detail view

ABUSE MITIGATIONS:
- IP rate limiting on comments and reactions
- Author can disable sharing at any time (link immediately 404s, comments preserved privately)
- Author can delete individual comments
- Optional: expiry date on share link
close_reason: Built: UUID share tokens, public /share/:token page with reactions (👍💡🔥) and flat comment thread, author reply/delete, unread badge on idea cards, ShareToggle in idea detail. IP-hashed rate limiting on comments (3/hr) and reactions. Toggle revokes old link on re-enable.

title: Browser extension - clip to viberater
description: Browser extension (Chrome/Firefox) to capture ideas without opening the app. Highlight text on any page and clip directly as a new idea. Pre-fills title from page title, summary from selected text, source URL in links. Auth via stored token. One-click save with optional quick edit before saving.

title: Rich attachments - links and images
description:
Add links and images to ideas, projects, and reminders.

LINKS:
- links[] field already exists in schema but UI is minimal
- Proper link cards with auto-fetched title/favicon/preview
- Add links from share sheet on mobile (Web Share Target)

IMAGES:
- Upload images directly to an idea (drag-drop or file picker)
- Paste from clipboard (screenshot workflow)
- Store on backend in /storage/attachments/
- Thumbnail preview on idea card
- Lightbox viewer

STORAGE:
- New attachments table: id, idea_id, user_id, type (image/link), url, metadata, created_at
- New backend route: POST /api/attachments (multipart upload), DELETE /api/attachments/:id
- Serve files via /api/attachments/:id/file

NOTE: sketching/canvas out of scope - use Obsidian integration for that.

title: Rename viberater to Hatch
description:
Full product rename from viberater to Hatch.

FILES TO UPDATE:
- backend/package.json: name field
- frontend/package.json: name field
- frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx: app title 'viberater' → 'Hatch'
- frontend/index.html: title tag
- docker-compose.yml: service names, container names
- k8s/namespace.yaml: namespace name
- k8s/configmap.yaml: name, CORS_ORIGIN, BACKEND_URL service names
- k8s/backend-deployment.yaml: deployment name, labels, service name
- k8s/frontend-deployment.yaml: deployment name, labels, service name
- k8s/backend-deployment-sqlite.yaml: same
- k8s/routes.yaml: host, service ref
- k8s/kustomization.yaml: references
- nginx-openshift.conf: any viberater refs
- All README/docs references
- GitHub repo name (manual)
- Container image paths in k8s (10.10.20.6:5000/nmerrett/viberater-* → hatch-*)

NOTE: k8s resources will need to be deleted and recreated (can't rename namespace in place). Plan a maintenance window.

title: AI code scaffolding and task suggestions
description: Pluggable code scaffolding system (Claude Code, Codex, watsonx). OUT OF CORE SCOPE - execution territory. Keep for future if viberater expands. Depends on git integration (viberater-bhm).

title: Project planning tool integrations
description:
Pluggable integration layer for syncing viberater projects and tasks with external planning tools.

INITIAL TARGET: Kanboard (self-hosted, JSON-RPC API)
FUTURE: Trello, Linear, GitHub Projects, GitLab Issues, Jira

SYNC MODEL:
- viberater project → Kanboard project
- viberater task → Kanboard card (with status mapping)
- Bidirectional or push-only (configurable per integration)
- Manual trigger or on save

ARCHITECTURE:
- Provider interface: connect(config), pushProject(project, tasks), pullTasks(projectId), syncStatus(taskId)
- Integration config stored per-project (provider, endpoint, auth token, project mapping)
- Same pluggable pattern as AI providers

KANBOARD SPECIFICS:
- JSON-RPC API at /jsonrpc.php
- Auth via API token or user:token header
- Methods: createProject, createTask, updateTask, getTasksByProject
- Map viberater task status (todo/in_progress/done) to Kanboard columns

UI:
- Integration settings per project (connect, configure, sync button)
- Sync status indicator on tasks (synced/pending/error)
- Conflict resolution: last-write-wins or manual

title: MCP server - expose Hatch to AI clients
description:
Implement Hatch as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so any MCP-compatible client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) can interact with it directly.

TOOLS TO EXPOSE:
- capture_idea: create a new idea with title, summary, tags, area
- search_ideas: search by keyword, tag, area, status
- get_idea: fetch a single idea by id
- list_ideas: list recent ideas with filters
- create_reminder: add a reminder with optional due date
- get_capture_thread: read recent capture conversation

USE CASES:
- Working in Claude Desktop: 'save this to Hatch' → creates idea
- In Cursor: 'what ideas do I have about auth?' → searches Hatch
- Any AI agent can use Hatch as a memory/capture layer

IMPLEMENTATION:
- New MCP server endpoint (SSE or stdio transport)
- Auth via API token (user generates in settings)
- Follow MCP spec: tools, resources, prompts
- Could be a separate lightweight process or built into backend

REFERENCE: modelcontextprotocol.io

title: Text-to-speech playback for AI responses
description: Auto-play or toggle TTS for AI messages in brainstorm chat. Use browser Web Speech API. Speaker toggle button, adjustable voice/rate.

title: Slash commands in capture chat
description: Slash command autocomplete popover in CaptureChat textarea. Arrow/Tab/Enter navigation. Commands: /idea [title], /remind [text], /note [text], /area [name], /ideas, /projects, /help. System messages rendered inline. batchGapSeconds setting in hamburger menu.

title: Database backups
description: Set up automated SQLite backups on OpenShift. CronJob to copy /app/storage/viberater.db to a backup PVC or object storage. Retention policy (e.g. 7 daily, 4 weekly).

title: Multi-device sync engine
description: Implement sync endpoints (stub exists at src/routes/sync.js). Conflict resolution strategy, device registration and tracking, sync queue for offline operations. IndexedDB layer already partially built in frontend.

title: Obsidian integration
description:
Export viberater content to an Obsidian vault as markdown files with YAML frontmatter.

EXPORT TARGETS:
- Ideas → markdown notes with frontmatter (tags, vibe, excitement, area, created_at)
- Projects → markdown with task checklist
- Reminders → Daily Notes or a reminders.md file
- Design documents → full markdown note

SYNC OPTIONS:
1. Local vault via filesystem path (self-hosted/desktop)
2. Obsidian Local REST API plugin (HTTP, requires plugin installed)
3. Webhook to a sync script (most flexible)

FRONTMATTER FORMAT:
---
title: Idea title
tags: [ai, web]
area: work
excitement: 8
created: 2026-05-03
source: viberater
---

VAULT STRUCTURE (configurable):
/viberater/ideas/
/viberater/projects/
/viberater/reminders/

DIRECTION: push-only initially (viberater → Obsidian). Bidirectional complex due to Obsidian's free-form editing.

NICE TO HAVE: backlink from Obsidian note back to viberater item via deep link.

title: Integration destination selector
description:
User manually selects which integration(s) to push each item to. Smart defaults pre-select the destination based on area/type, but user can always change or clear it.

BEHAVIOUR:
- Each item (idea/project/reminder) has an optional destination field (none, kanboard, obsidian, etc.)
- On create/edit: destination dropdown pre-populated based on area (e.g. area=work defaults to Kanboard, area=blog defaults to Obsidian) but fully overridable
- User can set default destination per area in settings (simple mapping, not a rules engine)
- Push happens on save or via explicit sync button on the item

UI:
- Small destination badge on cards (shows where it will sync)
- Dropdown in create/edit form
- Settings: per-area default destination mapping

DEPENDS ON: viberater-u0s (areas), viberater-4kg (Kanboard), viberater-83n (Obsidian)

title: Ollama provider (local LLM)
description: Add Ollama as a third AI provider option alongside Claude and OpenAI. Useful for offline/private usage. Backend already has provider abstraction.

title: Email monitoring with IMAP triage pipeline
description: Monitor inbox via IMAP. Stage 1: header hard-filters (List-Unsubscribe, Precedence, Auto-Submitted, noreply senders, known notification domains). Stage 2: subject regex rules (skip receipts/newsletters, flag follow-up/action keywords). Stage 3: body scan (unsubscribe link, word count bounds, question+action verb detection, date/deadline patterns). Only emails passing all stages go to LLM for reminder extraction. UI shows pending queue for user approval before reminders are saved.

title: Duplicate idea detection
description: Before saving a new idea, AI checks for semantic similarity against existing ideas and warns if a near-duplicate exists. Show the similar idea inline so user can merge, link, or proceed anyway. Run on create and optionally as a background scan across all ideas.

title: Auth security hardening
description: Rate limiting on auth routes (20 req/15min), bcrypt DoS protection (128 char password cap), email validation, input length limits, registration gate (ALLOW_REGISTRATION env flag), refresh token hashing (sha256 stored in DB), token rotation on refresh, 30-day TTL, daily expired token purge, CORS production lockdown.

title: Clipboard paste attachment in capture chat
description: Allow pasting images from clipboard directly into the capture chat. Image is held as a pending attachment and linked to the most recently captured idea from that conversation session. If no idea has been captured yet in the session, prompt the user to capture an idea first or attach it to an existing idea via a picker.

title: Conversational AI capture - persistent thread
description:
Primary capture experience as a single persistent conversation thread per user. Feels like messaging a smart friend - pick up mid-thought days later and context is still there.

EXPERIENCE:
- First tab in the app (default landing screen)
- One continuous mixed thread - work, blog, personal all together, that's how you actually think
- Voice notes: hold to record, release to send - AI transcribes and responds
- AI detects new idea vs continuing previous thread
- AI references past captures: 'this sounds related to your homelab idea from last week'
- 2-3 message exchange naturally extracts title, summary, tags without a form
- AI prompts for area classification during conversation - infers from context then confirms: 'this sounds like a blog thing - want me to file it there?'
- Quick confirmation before saving, fully editable after

AREA CLASSIFICATION:
- AI infers area from context clues (mentions work/client → work, homelab/blog → blog, etc.)
- Always asks rather than silently assumes: 'filing this under Personal - right?'
- If no strong signal: 'which area does this belong to?' with user's area list as options
- User can just reply 'yeah' or 'no, work' - keeps it conversational

CONVERSATION HISTORY:
- Full message history retained for a configurable period (default 60 days)
- Older messages summarised and compressed, recent messages kept verbatim
- Timestamps visible so you can see when you were discussing something

IDEA LINKBACK:
- Ideas captured from the thread link back to the specific messages that spawned them
- 'View conversation' on an idea opens the thread at that point
- Useful for context - why did I think this was a good idea?

SCHEMA:
- capture_messages: id, user_id, role (user/assistant), content, created_at
- ideas.capture_message_id FK → capture_messages (the message where idea was saved)
- history_retention_days: user setting, default 60

AI CONTEXT:
- Recent N messages sent verbatim
- Older messages replaced with a rolling summary
- AI has list of previously captured ideas and user's defined areas for cross-referencing

TECHNICAL:
- Lightweight chat component, first tab, always mounted
- Separate from BrainstormChat (that is deep refinement of an existing idea)
- Short punchy system prompt focused on capture not exploration
- After saving an idea: 'Saved ✓' inline, conversation continues naturally
close_reason: Fully built: persistent thread, streaming SSE, area inference, multi-idea detection per message, offline message queuing with time-based batching, slash commands. Missing: rolling summary compression for old messages (uses hard limit instead) and voice input in capture chat specifically.

title: Git integration - repo management
description: Git repo management - init/clone, commit/push/pull, status/diff, SSH keys. OUT OF CORE SCOPE - execution territory, not capture/initiation. Keep for future consideration if viberater ever expands into a dev workflow tool.

title: Monitoring and logging
description: Add Prometheus metrics endpoint to backend. Deploy Grafana dashboard for request rates, error rates, response times. Set up log aggregation (Loki or similar). OpenShift-native options preferred.

title: Messaging bot capture - Signal, WhatsApp, Telegram
description:
Capture ideas and reminders by messaging a bot in your existing messaging apps. Zero-friction capture from apps you're already using.

USE CASES:
- Text message → new idea
- Voice note → transcribed and saved as idea
- Photo → attached to new idea
- 'remind me to X tomorrow' → creates reminder
- Bot replies with confirmation + deep link back to item

PROVIDERS (easiest to hardest):
1. Telegram - official Bot API, free, no business account, easiest to self-host
2. WhatsApp - Meta Cloud API, requires Meta Business account, phone number verification
3. Signal - signal-cli daemon, most private, no official bot API, more complex

BACKEND:
- Webhook receiver per provider
- Message parser: detect intent (idea vs reminder), extract content
- Voice note handling: download + transcribe via existing AI transcription
- Reply with confirmation

RECOMMENDED START: Telegram bot - 1 hour to get running, proper API, works great for self-hosted. User can always add WhatsApp/Signal later.

AUTH: link bot to account via one-time code sent through the app.

title: Email-to-idea forwarding address
description: Each user gets a unique forwarding address (e.g. nick+viberater@ideas.domain.com). Email sent to it lands as a new idea. Subject becomes title, body becomes summary. AI optionally cleans up and extracts tags/area. Simple SMTP receiver or IMAP polling on a dedicated mailbox.

title: Idea scoring - effort vs impact matrix
description: Score ideas on effort (1-5) and impact (1-5) in addition to excitement/complexity. 2x2 matrix view showing all ideas plotted by effort vs impact. Quick wins (low effort, high impact) highlighted. Helps prioritise which ideas to act on.

title: Incubation timer - resurface ideas
description: Mark an idea as 'incubating' with a duration (1 week, 2 weeks, 1 month). After the period a notification/reminder fires to revisit it with fresh eyes. Removes ideas from the active list while incubating so they don't clutter the view.

title: Streaming AI responses
description: Stream Claude/OpenAI responses token-by-token in BrainstormChat and AIChat components instead of waiting for full response. Improves perceived performance significantly.

title: Weekly review mode
description: Dedicated review flow that surfaces ideas untouched for 2+ weeks. Presents each one with a simple triage: keep active, archive, promote to project, or snooze. Tracks last_reviewed_at. Could be triggered manually or via a weekly notification. GTD-style weekly review.

title: A2A agent - Hatch as a delegatable agent
description:
Implement Hatch as an A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol agent so other AI agents can delegate capture and retrieval tasks to it.

CAPABILITIES:
- Receive capture requests from other agents
- Answer knowledge queries ('what do I know about X?')
- Surface relevant ideas given a context
- Create reminders on behalf of other agents

USE CASES:
- Orchestrator agent delegates memory storage to Hatch agent
- Email processing agent sends action items to Hatch
- Any agentic workflow that needs a personal knowledge store

REFERENCE: Google A2A protocol spec
NOTE: A2A less mature than MCP - implement MCP first, A2A once ecosystem stabilises.

title: Split idea feature
description: SplitIdeaModal: AI suggests split into 2 ideas from original title/summary/notes. User can edit both halves, re-request suggestion. On confirm: creates 2 new ideas inheriting tags/area/vibe/complexity, archives original. ✂️ Split button on each active idea card.

title: One-click blog post draft from idea
description: Button on any idea to generate a full blog post draft using AI. Uses idea title, summary, design document, and notes as context. Output as markdown. Preview in app, copy to clipboard, or push directly to Obsidian (area=blog default). Configurable tone/length/format.

title: Export to markdown, PDF, Notion
description: Export ideas, projects, or reminders to markdown (download), PDF (generated server-side), or push to Notion via API. Markdown export most useful - structured frontmatter + body. Notion integration via unofficial/official API with configurable target database.

title: Areas - user-defined context classification
description:
GTD-style areas/spaces that classify all content into user-defined buckets (e.g. work, home, personal, inverloch, blog).

SCHEMA:
- areas table: id, user_id, name, color, icon, sort_order, created_at
- Add area_id (nullable FK) to: ideas, reminders, projects, tasks

BACKEND:
- migrations/007_add_areas.sql
- src/routes/areas.js (CRUD: list, create, update, delete, reorder)
- On first login bootstrap default areas: Work, Home, Personal, Blog
- Update ideas/reminders/projects/tasks routes to accept area_id on create and update

FRONTEND:
- Areas management in user settings (add, rename, reorder, color pick, delete)
- Area filter pill row in dashboard - filters across all tabs (ideas, projects, reminders)
- Area badge on all cards
- Area selector in create/edit forms for ideas, reminders, projects
- Default filter is 'All' (no area scoping)
- Search/filter works across all areas by default

DEFAULTS (bootstrapped per user on register):
Work (#3b82f6), Home (#22c55e), Personal (#a855f7), Blog (#f97316)
User can rename, reorder, add, delete.

title: Background sync + PWA update prompt
description: Switched to injectManifest SW strategy. Custom sw.js with Workbox. Background Sync API (SyncManager) registers viberater-sync tag when operations queued offline — SW notifies clients on reconnect. Fallback: window online event. PWA update prompt (registerType: prompt) with SKIP_WAITING message. UpdatePrompt component shows banner on new SW available.

title: Idea graph - visual connection map
description: Visual graph/map showing connections between ideas (parent_idea_id, related_ideas). Force-directed graph using a canvas library (e.g. d3 or cytoscape). Click node to open idea. Colour by area or status. Reveals clusters and orphaned ideas.
